tool_args.label: Back-cooking modification nearly eliminates fare evasion at turnstiles
tool_args.raw_text: At the turnstiles, we have been scaling up solutions that work for months. It started with the in-house modification to eliminate back-cooking, which is now implemented at nearly 90% of all turnstiles. This modification has nearly eliminated instances of back-cooking where installed, and we plan to install it at all turnstiles by the summer.

payload.claim_text: The in-house modification to eliminate back-cooking is now implemented at nearly 90% of all turnstiles and has nearly eliminated instances of back-cooking where installed

tool_args.label: Subway fare evasion down 26% in latter half of 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Fare evasion on subways was down 26% in the latter half of 2024

payload.claim_text: Fare evasion on subways was down 26% in the latter half of 2024

tool_args.label: Emergency Gate Delay Installation
tool_args.raw_text: At the same time, we are expanding modifications to infrastructure to limit the rate of gate evasion. By installing a delay to the emergency gates at three pilot stations in 2024, we were able to observe their effectiveness and map out a plan to expand this intervention in 2025. We have begun to scale this intervention up in recent months and have committed to have gate delays operational at 150 stations by the end of 2025.

payload.description: Installation of delays to emergency gates to limit gate evasion, committed to have gate delays operational at 150 stations by end of 2025

tool_args.label: Unarmed Gate Guards at Exit Gates
tool_args.raw_text: The exit gates have long been the superhighway of fare evasion. In the Blue-Ribbon Panel's report, it was estimated that roughly half of all fare evasion occurs through these gates. To stem this source of evasion, the MTA has been deploying unarmed guards at exit gates. Their presence reduces unnecessary usage of these gates and limits opportunities for evasion. Gate guards are currently deployed at over 200 stations

payload.description: Unarmed guards deployed at exit gates to reduce fare evasion

tool_args.label: Eagle Teams Fare Enforcement on Buses
tool_args.raw_text: On buses, we continue to scale and refine the deployment of our Eagle Teams to enforce fare payment onboard. Since this new deployment strategy was introduced in Q3 of 2024, fare evasion has gone down precipitously. Currently, Eagle Teams are interacting with roughly 1,500 buses every single day, with more Eagle Teams on the way later this year.

payload.description: Eagle Teams deployed to enforce fare payment onboard buses, interacting with roughly 1,500 buses per day
